semanticCore: Poison, eye of a needle, scorching wind, penetration, or narrow hole
primaryMeaning: Scorching wind or eye of a needle
quranicRelevance: Camel passing through needle eye (Summ - 'sammi al-khiyati'), and scorching wind of Hell (Samoom - 'fee samoomin', 'athaba as-samoomi').

keyDerivations.meaning: Eye of a needle, narrow hole, channel, or piercing exit
Scorching wind, piercing fire-heat, thermal storm, or poison wind

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَلَا يَدْخُلُونَ الْجَنَّةَ حَتَّىٰ يَلِجَ الْجَمَلُ فِي سَمِّ الْخِيَاطِ (7:40): Nor will they enter Paradise until the camel passes through the eye of the needle (mathematical/physical impossibility of arrogance entering heaven).
summaryEn: The root connects narrow penetrations with intense heat, describing the tiny needle-eye (Summ) camel metaphor and the piercing scorching heat (Samoom) of Hell.
summaryAr: يربط هذا الجذر بين الاختراق والضيق الشديد (سم الخياط)، والحرارة النافذة للمسام (السموم)، واصفاً استحالة دخول المتكبر للجنة وعذاب السموم.
rawText: سَاسَمٌ , without ء, (AHát, TA,) A kind of tree, of which arrows are made: (AHát, M:) a kind of black tree: (S, K:) or a kind of tree (AHn, M, K) of the mountains, of the [sort termed] عُتُق, (AHn, M,) of which bows are made: (AHn, M, K:) or, (K,) as some assert, (AHn, M,) the [tree called] آبِنُوس [i. e. ebony]: (AHn, M, K:) or, (K,) as others assert, (AHn, M,) the [tree called] شِيز: (AHn, M, K: [in some copies of the K شِيزَى, which means the same:]) but neither of these two is suitable for bows. (AHn, M.)
